revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Isagu-jinja]][[ja:伊佐具神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Isagu Shrine | native_name = 伊佐具神社 | image = Isagujinja01.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Isagu Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.748617|135.431554}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Isagu Shrine'''|伊佐具神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver|lt=Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver|qid=Q135009152|1=Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11498550|ltq=Q11498550|qid=Q11498550|1=List of Shikinaisha in Settsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 40 ** P155: {{ill|Ahita Shrine|lt=Ahita Shrine|ja|阿比太神社|qid=Q11657481|1=Ahita Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q11669105|ltq=Q11669105|qid=Q11669105|1=Takamefu Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q213318|ltq=Q213318|qid=Q213318|1=Amagasaki}} * {{ill|Settsu Province|lt=Settsu Province|ca|Província de Settsu|cs|Provincie Seccu|de|Provinz Settsu|en|Settsu Province|eo|Provinco Seccu|es|Provincia de Settsu|fa|ولایت ستسو|fr|Province de Settsu|gl|Provincia de Settsu|hak|Settsu-koet|id|Provinsi Settsu|it|Settsu (provincia)|ja|摂津国|ko|셋쓰국|lt|Seccu žemė|nl|Settsu (provincie)|pt|Província de Settsu|ru|Сэтцу (провинция)|simple|Settsu Province|sk|Seccu (provincia)|sr|Провинција Сецу|tr|Settsu (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Сеццу|zh_min_nan|Settu-kok|zh_yue|攝津國|zh|攝津國|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} * {{ill|Q1042053|ltq=Q1042053|qid=Q1042053|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.748617|135.431554}} == image (P18) == * Isagujinja01.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181060 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いさぐじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6140005011279 == Commons category (P373) == * Isagu-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県尼崎市上坂部3丁目25-18', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Isagu-jinja}} [[JA:伊佐具神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11378870}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Isagu Shrine |Image = [[File:Isagu-jinja, keidai-3.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Amagasaki City|Q213318|qid=Q213318|1=Amagasaki}} Kamisakabe 3-chōme 25-18 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|44|55.4|N|135|25|53|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Ikisashiniri-hiko no Mikoto and two other unidentified deities |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = |Main Hall = |Alternate name = |Annual festival = October 22 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Isagu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} {{Osm box|n|1807231476}} '''Isagu Shrine''' (いさぐじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kamisakabe, {{ill|lt=Amagasaki City|Q213318|qid=Q213318|1=Amagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Ikisashiniri-hiko no Mikoto and two other unidentified deities * Associated shrines: Miyahachimangū, Konpira Shrine, Atago Shrine, Tenman-gūInformation board at the shrine === History === The ''{{ill|lt=Settsu Meisho Zue|Q11498549|qid=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}}'' states: “Located in Kamisakabe Village. Listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}''. Now called {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Myōjin|1=UNKNOWN}}. Together with Morimura, it serves as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q1392097|qid=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}}.” Among the seven {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} shrines of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|qid=Q1042053|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, this is the only shrine with the distinguished tradition of having {{ill|lt=hoes|Q131154|qid=Q131154|1=Hoe (tool)}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=quivers|1=UNKNOWN}} dedicated to it by the Imperial Court. Furthermore, in “{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Genbaryō|Q11572314|qid=Q11572314|1=Genbaryō}} Volume 21,” it is recorded that this shrine was one of twelve shrines selected from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu|1=Settsu Province}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi|1=Kawachi Province}}, and {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|qid=Q1047117|lt=Izumi|1=Izumi Province}} to send rice to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} for brewing {{ill|lt=sacred sake|Q3313722|qid=Q3313722|1=Omiki}} to be bestowed upon envoys from {{ill|lt=Silla|Q28456|qid=Q28456|1=Silla}}. In June of {{ill|lt=Genkō|Q1194556|qid=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} 3 (1333), it is said that {{ill|lt=Akamatsu Norimura|Q2828933|qid=Q2828933|lt=Akamatsu Enshin|1=Akamatsu Norimura}} established a {{ill|lt=military camp|Q11658173|qid=Q11658173|1=Position}} near this shrine and prayed here for victory in battle. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.Sadatoshi Yoshii, “Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 5” (1977), {{National Bibliography Number|78005760}} === Precincts === * Isagu Shrine name-inscribed stone marker – A shrine marker identified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} by {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|qid=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}}. The front bears the inscription “Isagu-sha.” It is designated a Tangible Cultural Property of Amagasaki City. * {{ill|lt=Gorintō|Q304906|qid=Q304906|1=Gorintō}} – A stone pagoda dating to around the {{ill|lt=Gen’ō|Q1193925|qid=Q1193925|1=Gen'ō}} era (1319–1321), said to be the grave of Akamatsu Enshin. * Hakuryū Inari Shrine – Uses the hall of the former temple Jōtoku-ji of the Shingon sect, retaining traces of syncretism between Shinto and Buddhism. * Hachiman Shrine File:Isagu-jinja, sekihi.jpg|Shrine name-inscribed stone marker (designated cultural property of Amagasaki City) File:Isagu-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Hakuryū Inari Shrine File:Isagu-jinja, torii-2.jpg|Torii gate === Cultural properties === === Cultural properties designated by Amagasaki City === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Isagu Shrine name-inscribed stone marker (historical material) – Designated on April 1, 1988 (Shōwa 63)[http://www2.city.amagasaki.hyogo.jp/bunkazai/sitei/isagu-hyouseki/isagu-hyouseki.html Isagu Shrine name-inscribed stone marker] (Amagasaki City website). === Access === * 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Tsukaguchi Station (JR West)|Q847972|qid=Q847972|lt=Tsukaguchi Station|1=Tsukaguchi Station (JR West)}} on the {{ill|lt=Fukuchiyama Line|Q1154538|qid=Q1154538|1=Fukuchiyama Line}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|qid=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Isagu-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Isagu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Isagu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Isagu Shrine]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Amagasaki]] [[Category:尼崎市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Sonoda district]]
